prompt:
Garden club rain-gauge network, the reporting half. Readings come in as plain numeric arrays (mm, ints or floats, negatives possible once the snow-melt correction runs) and the weekly email is plain text, so: unicode charts, and byte-stable output — the email diff is how we spot sensor drift, so the SAME series must render the SAME string forever. I already worked out the arithmetic; do not improvise your own rounding.

Module `RainReport` in `rain_report.rb`, two functions.

`RainReport.sparkline(values)` → one string, one bar per reading, bars ▁▂▃▄▅▆▇█ (lowest to highest).
- empty array → ""
- span = max - min; span zero (all readings equal, or a single reading) → every bar is ▁
- otherwise each value maps to bar index ((v - min) * 7.0 / span).round — EXACTLY that expression: multiply by 7.0 first, THEN divide, then Float#round. Order matters: it keeps exact halves exact, and Float#round takes halves AWAY from zero (a value landing on 4.5 is bar index 5). Do it another way and the tie cases in the tests will bite you.

`RainReport.histogram(values, buckets:, width:)` → a labeled multi-line string, one line per bucket, joined with \n, NO trailing newline.
- guards first, ArgumentError, messages exactly: "no data" (empty values), "buckets must be >= 1, got 0", "width must be >= 1, got -2" (whatever was passed)
- lo/hi = min/max; span = hi - lo. Value v lands in bucket ((v - lo) * buckets / span.to_f).floor, clamped to the last bucket (so v == hi counts in bucket buckets-1). A value sitting exactly on an interior boundary therefore opens the NEXT bucket. span zero → everything lands in bucket 0.
- bucket i is labeled "LO..HI" where LO = lo + span * i / buckets.to_f and HI is the same for i+1, both through format("%.1f", x). Labels right-justified to the widest label.
- each line is: label, space, pipe, the bar, space, the count — like "9.0..12.0 |██████ 3". The bar is █ repeated: zero-count buckets get NO bar chars (line reads "label | 0"), any nonzero count gets at least one █, otherwise length is (count * width / max_count.to_f).round where max_count is the biggest bucket count (so the fullest bucket spans the full width).
- empty buckets are still rendered — a gap in the distribution should LOOK like a gap.

Acceptance is `test_rain_report.rb`, sitting right here — expected strings are pinned character-for-character, including the tie, negative, all-equal, and single-element series. Hands off the tests. `ruby test_rain_report.rb` exits clean when you're done.
